questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao served as Prime Minister of India during which period?
questions.explanation: P.V. Narasimha Rao served as Prime Minister from 1991 to 1996.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's tenure is most closely associated with which major change in India?
questions.explanation: His tenure presided over the economic liberalisation of India.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao is given which descriptive title on the Katha Cards deck?
questions.explanation: He is titled 'Architect of Reform' on the deck.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao is noted as the first Prime Minister from which part of India to serve a full term?
questions.explanation: He was the first Prime Minister from South India to serve a full term.

questions.q: In which year did P.V. Narasimha Rao become Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: He became Prime Minister in 1991.

questions.q: The economic reforms during P.V. Narasimha Rao's tenure began in which year?
questions.explanation: The economic liberalisation reforms began in 1991, at the start of his tenure.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's tenure as Prime Minister lasted approximately how long?
questions.explanation: He served a full term of about five years, from 1991 to 1996.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao became Prime Minister immediately after which leader's tenure?
questions.explanation: He became Prime Minister in 1991, succeeding Chandra Shekhar.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms during P.V. Narasimha Rao's tenure opened the economy to which of the following?
questions.explanation: The reforms opened the economy to private and foreign investment.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's premiership is described as which kind of tenure in terms of completion?
options: An interim caretaker term of weeks
A term cut short within months
questions.explanation: He is noted for serving a full term as Prime Minister.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao is often described as which sequential Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: P.V. Narasimha Rao is counted as the ninth Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: The 1991 economic reforms were introduced in the context of which kind of crisis?
questions.explanation: The reforms were introduced amid a balance-of-payments crisis.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's government came to power following which event?
questions.explanation: His government came to power following the 1991 general election.

questions.q: Which leader headed the United Front government that took office in 1996 after P.V. Narasimha Rao's term?
questions.explanation: H.D. Deve Gowda headed the United Front government that took office in 1996 following P.V. Narasimha Rao's term.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's tenure is regarded as a turning point chiefly in which area of national policy?
questions.explanation: His tenure is regarded as a turning point in economic policy through the 1991 liberalisation.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms during P.V. Narasimha Rao's tenure broadly did which of the following?
options: Liberalised trade and reduced state controls
questions.explanation: The reforms liberalised trade and reduced state controls over the economy.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's premiership fell within which broader phase of Indian politics?
options: The era of coalition and minority governments from the late 1980s
questions.explanation: His government governed during the era of coalition and minority politics that began in the late 1980s.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's distinction of serving a full term from South India is measured against earlier Prime Ministers who were predominantly from where?
questions.explanation: Earlier Prime Ministers were predominantly from northern India, making his full term a first for the south.

questions.q: The Finance Minister most associated with implementing the 1991 reforms under P.V. Narasimha Rao was who?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh, as Finance Minister, led the 1991 economic reforms under P.V. Narasimha Rao.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao formally assumed the premiership through which constitutional act?
questions.explanation: He was appointed Prime Minister by the President, as the Constitution requires.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's term ran from 1991 to which year?
questions.explanation: His term ran from 1991 to 1996.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's home region in the south corresponds to which present-day area?
options: The Telugu-speaking region of the Deccan
questions.explanation: He hailed from the Telugu-speaking region of the Deccan in South India.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's premiership immediately followed that of which short-tenured Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: His premiership immediately followed that of Chandra Shekhar.

questions.q: The 1991 reforms are described on the deck as reforms that opened the economy amid which specific economic pressure?
options: A trade embargo by all neighbours
questions.explanation: The reforms were introduced amid a balance-of-payments crisis in 1991.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's full-term distinction is significant because it broke a pattern of what in the immediately preceding years?
options: A series of long single-party majorities
An absence of any Prime Minister
A run of presidential rule at the Union
questions.explanation: His full term broke a pattern of short-lived governments in the immediately preceding years.

questions.q: The Finance Minister who delivered the landmark 1991 reform budget under P.V. Narasimha Rao later himself became which office-holder?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh, his Finance Minister in 1991, later served as Prime Minister from 2004 to 2014.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's economic policy shift is often contrasted with the earlier framework characterised by what?
questions.explanation: His reforms moved away from the earlier framework of extensive state controls and licensing.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao led a government that, despite reforms, operated within which constitutional reality of its time?
options: The need to maintain a Lok Sabha majority or working support
questions.explanation: His government had to maintain a Lok Sabha majority or working support to govern.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's status as the first full-term Prime Minister from South India places him in contrast with which earlier southern leaders' shorter or later tenures?
options: Leaders before 1991 from the south who did not complete full Union terms
questions.explanation: He was the first from the south to complete a full Union term, unlike earlier southern leaders.

questions.q: P.V. Narasimha Rao's tenure connects two landmarks on the deck: the 1991 economic liberalisation and his position as the ninth Prime Minister. Which statement is accurate?
options: He was the seventh Prime Minister and opposed the reforms
He was the ninth Prime Minister and presided over the 1991 reforms
He was the eleventh Prime Minister and reversed the reforms
He was the fifth Prime Minister and predated the reforms
questions.explanation: He was the ninth Prime Minister and presided over the 1991 economic liberalisation.
